data.title: Allarity regains compliance with NASDAQ minimum bid price requirement
data.description:
• Allarity Therapeutics (NASDAQ:ALLR) has announced that on Oct. 9, 2024, the Co. has regained compliance with the minimum bid price requirement as set forth in Nasdaq’s Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • Nasdaq noted that since Sept. 11, 2024, Allarity’s stock has maintained a closing bid price of $1.00 or more for 20 consecutive trading days, thereby meeting the requirements for regaining compliance with the Bid Price Rule.

data.title: Tanger declares $0.275 dividend
data.description:
• Tanger (NYSE:SKT) declared $0.275/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.26%
  • Payable Nov. 15; for shareholders of record Oct. 31; ex-div Oct. 31.
  • See SKT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: TeraWulf enters into ground lease agreement with Somerset Operating Company
data.description:
• Digital asset technology company TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) signed a long-term ground lease agreement with Somerset Operating Company at Lake Mariner.
  • The ground lease is expected to support the company's expansion into high-performance computing and AI data centers.
  • WULF shares were trading +2.09% Thursday pre-market at $3.90.
  • The ground lease has a term of 35 years, with an option to extend for an additional 45 years, and increases the Lake Mariner land area by nearly 50%, expanding from 107 acres to 157 acres.
  • The new lease replaces the May 2021 Lake Mariner lease, which had ten years remaining.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sunnova gains as Jefferies eyes potential turnaround, initiates at Buy
data.description:
Sunnova Energy (NYSE:NOVA) jumped over 7% in premarket trading on Thursday, as brokerage Jefferies initiated its coverage on the solar installer with a Buy rating and $15 price target.

"We think NOVA's story is sorely misunderstood and initiate with a Buy with the view that the stock will rerate once the company proves that guidance is indeed actionable," said analysts at Jefferies.

During its second-quarter earnings call, Sunnova made substantial increases to its cash generation from cash-neutral to an estimated $100 million in 2024. NOVA also upped cash generation guidance for 2025 and 2026 to $350 million and $400 million, respectively.

The brokerage added that the increase in cash generation should reassure investors who questioned how exactly NOVA would generate the cash to address the $2 bn of maturities coming up in 2026-2028.

While NOVA's peers in the industry are expecting solar shipments/installations to be down in 2024, Jefferies commented that it is "impressive" that NOVA is signaling the opposite; guiding 2024 solar MWs up 25% y/y.

"We suspect that investors largely remain hesitant regarding cash generation guidance and view the upcoming election as a considerable risk, but we believe investors are placing too much weight on a potential IRA repeal that we view as unlikely," said Jefferies.

Since the start of the year, Sunnova Energy has fallen about 55%. Seeking Alpha's Quant has rated the company as a Hold, while sell-side analysts recommend NOVA as a Buy.

data.title: Raytheon wins $736M contract for AIM-9X Sidewinder missiles
data.description:
• Raytheon, a unit of RTX (NYSE:RTX), has been awarded a U.S. Navy contract worth $736M to produce the improved Block II variant of the AIM-9X Sidewinder missiles.
  • The AIM-9X is used for short-range air-to-air and surface-to-air missile defense by the U.S. and over 30 allied and partner nations.
  • Work under the latest contract will take place in various locations in the U.S. through 2029.

data.title: Kezar receives acquisition proposal from Concentra for $1.10 per share
data.description:
• Biotech company, Kezar Life Sciences (NASDAQ:KZR) said on Thursday that it has received an unsolicited, non-binding proposal from Concentra Biosciences, to acquire all the outstanding shares of Kezar for cash consideration of $1.10 per share.
  • The proposal also includes a contingent value right that represents the right to receive 80% of the net proceeds from any out-license or disposition of Kezar’s development programs or intellectual property.
  • Kezar (KZR) said its board and management team will carefully evaluate the non-binding proposal.
  • The company’s shares rose more than 6% in pre-market trading on Thursday.

data.title: Stock index futures fall September CPI rises more than expected
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday, as September consumer inflation comes in hotter than expected.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.3% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.07%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.02%.

Major U.S. stock averages closed in the green on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Dow (DJI) ending at new peaks after the Federal Reserve's September meeting minutes said a "substantial majority" on its rate-setting board backed its big half-point cut to start an easing cycle.

"Markets have put in another strong performance over the last 24 hours, as optimism about the near-term economic outlook continues to build," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"Reading the (FOMC) Minutes, CPI is important to what happens next," Economist Steven Blitz from Global Data TS Lombard said.

September Consumer Price Index +0.2% M/M vs. +0.1% expected and +0.2% prior, +2.4% Y/Y vs. +2.3% expected and +2.5% prior.

Core CPI, which excludes food and energy: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.2% expected and +0.3% prior, +3.3% Y/Y vs. +3.2% expected and +3.2% prior.

The CPI report is in particular focus after the strong jobs report last Friday, as there’s been growing speculation that the Fed might not cut at all at their next meeting in November, Allen added.

The initial jobless claims climbed more than expected in the past week.

The Fed balance sheet will come later in the day.

data.title: YY Group regains compliance with minimum bid price rule
data.description:
• YY Group (NASDAQ:YYGH) said the company has regained compliance with the Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule.
  • Press Release

data.title: September CPI rises more than expected, indicating inflation risks remain
data.description:
• The progress of disinflation appeared to stall in September as the Consumer Price Index rose +0.2% M/M, stronger than the +0.1% expected and flat with August's +0.2% increase, according to U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data released on Thursday.
  • Headline CPI increased 2.4% Y/Y vs. +2.3% expected and +2.5% prior.
  • Inflation in core CPI, which excludes food and energy, also stalled, rising +0.3% M/M vs. +0.2% expected and +0.3% prior.
  • +3.3% Y/Y vs. +3.2% expected and +3.2% prior.
  • Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Arcadium Lithium downgraded at HSBC after Rio Tinto's $6.7B bid
data.description:
Arcadium Lithium (NYSE:ALTM) on Thursday was downgraded to Hold from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at financial-services firm HSBC. They said the lithium miner is fairly valued after its board approved a $6.7 billion all-cash acquisition offer by Rio Tinto (RIO).

Shares of Arcadium (ALTM) surged 31% to a nine-month high on Wednesday after the deal was announced.

“Though the sale agreement occurs at what we believe to be the trough of the cycle, the valuation seems fair given near-term risks to capacity and a possible equity dilution and/or leveraged balance sheet had the company raised additional financing,” Santhosh Seshadri, analyst at HSBC, said in an October 10 report.

The risks that the deal will fall through are minimal, according to the bank.

“We don’t see antitrust hurdles to deal completion as Rio Tinto (RIO) is not a lithium-producing company,” HSBC said. “Supermajority shareholder vote (75%) requires the blessings of Blackrock and Vanguard that cumulatively own a roughly 20% stake. The scope for counteroffer seems limited, given likely limited interest from most diversified miners.”

Lithium is a key ingredient in rechargeable batteries, including those for electric vehicles.
